summary: Frontend-only verification of two new delete affordances (editor delete-book-btn and write-tab draft-delete-{index}). Static review confirmed correct imports (deleteBook, Alert) and correct logic (deleteBook → router.replace('/') in editor; deleteBook → load() in write tab). Both buttons render, are visible, are properly sized for touch, have trash icons, and use the correct testIDs. Event propagation between draft-delete-{index} and the parent draft-row TouchableOpacity is correctly isolated — tapping the trash does NOT navigate to the editor. End-to-end functional testing of the destructive Alert callback is NOT possible on the web preview because react-native-web's Alert.alert is a no-op (`static alert() {}` in node_modules/react-native-web/dist/exports/Alert/index.js — confirmed). The reviewer note in the request that 'Alert.alert maps to window.confirm' is INCORRECT for this app — there is no Alert polyfill installed. On the actual Android target platform (the only supported platform per the problem statement), the destructive onPress callback will fire and deleteBook will be invoked, which is what the user originally complained was missing.

ui_bugs.issue: References styles.btnDanger and styles.btnDangerText but these keys are NOT defined in the StyleSheet.create at the bottom of the file (only btnPrimary/btnPrimaryText, btnGhost/btnGhostText exist). Effect: button renders but with no red background and the 'Delete book' label uses default text color instead of red. Functionally still tappable (verified: visible:true, 348x46, opacity:1, text 'Delete book' with trash icon).

integration_issues.issue: react-native-web v0.21 Alert.alert is implemented as `static alert() {}` (no-op). Therefore on the web preview, tapping delete-book-btn or draft-delete-0 invokes Alert.alert but the destructive button onPress NEVER fires. Result: no deletion happens on web. This is a platform limitation, NOT a bug in the new code. On Android (target), Alert.alert renders the native dialog and the Delete button's onPress callback runs deleteBook() correctly.

action_items: OPTIONAL (cosmetic): Add `btnDanger: { backgroundColor: 'rgba(255,107,107,0.10)', borderWidth: 1, borderColor: 'rgba(255,107,107,0.35)' }` and `btnDangerText: { color: '#ff6b6b', fontWeight: '600', fontSize: 15 }` to the StyleSheet in /app/frontend/app/editor/[id].tsx to make the 'Delete book' button visually red as intended. Currently the icon is red but the label/background are not.

VERIFY ON DEVICE: Since web preview cannot execute Alert.alert callbacks (react-native-web limitation), the actual delete-row-disappearing behavior must be confirmed on an Android emulator/Expo Go before declaring the user-reported bug fixed. Code is statically correct.

NOTE FOR FUTURE TESTING: The reviewer-supplied note 'Alert.alert maps to window.confirm on web' is not true for this codebase — react-native-web 0.21 ships Alert as a no-op. If web e2e of Alert flows is required, an Alert polyfill (e.g., a small wrapper using window.confirm) would need to be added.
critical_code_review_comments: editor/[id].tsx imports `deleteBook` from storage and `Alert` from react-native — verified at lines 13 and 22.
Both files compile cleanly; Metro bundle log shows successful build.

draft-delete-{index} TouchableOpacity is nested inside the row TouchableOpacity. Verified at runtime that tapping the trash does NOT trigger the parent onPress (URL stays on /write, no navigation to /editor). Event isolation works correctly on RN-web — likely because RN-web TouchableOpacity uses pointer events that Pressable hierarchy in RN-web handles properly.
deleteBook in storage.ts correctly filters out the book by id and persists via AsyncStorage — implementation is straightforward and correct.
Cosmetic miss: styles.btnDanger and styles.btnDangerText are referenced but undefined; React Native silently ignores undefined style keys.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on what can be verified on web (button rendering, testIDs, layout, event isolation, touch targets); end-to-end Alert callback NOT verifiable on web preview due to RN-web Alert no-op

seed_data_creation: Cleared @ebook/* keys from localStorage to start fresh; created an Untitled draft via new-draft-btn during verification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: react-native-web 0.21 Alert.alert is a no-op (static alert() {} in node_modules). Web preview cannot trigger destructive onPress callbacks. To functionally test delete flows on web, either (a) add an Alert polyfill that maps to window.confirm and reroutes to the destructive button's onPress, or (b) test on an Android emulator. All testIDs delete-book-btn, draft-delete-{index}, draft-row-{index}, editor-meta, editor-close, new-draft-btn, tab-write are present and tappable.
rca_of_issue: Original user complaint: 'cannot remove a draft'. Root cause was lack of an explicit delete affordance — the only deletion path was a long-press on Library cards which is not discoverable for drafts in the Write tab. Mitigation in iteration 3: (1) Added an explicit trash icon button per draft row with testID draft-delete-{index} (write.tsx). (2) Added a 'Delete book' button in the editor meta sheet with testID delete-book-btn (editor/[id].tsx). Both invoke Alert.alert with a destructive 'Delete' option that calls deleteBook(id) and either reloads the drafts list (write.tsx) or router.replace('/')-s back to the library (editor). Static code is correct; functional verification on Android is required since RN-web Alert.alert is a no-op.
